creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel promotes AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expanded content, an expo floor, research-industry collaboration, leadership tracks, AI vertical focus, and community events. The video includes promotional offers and a YouTube-exclusive discount code.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents a production AI playbook for deploying agents at enterprise scale. The framework is built on five pillars: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk draws from a retail bank engagement where tracing infrastructure caught that a stale knowledge base caused customer satisfaction drops after a policy update.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: The talk examines how agents fail when accessing the open web, pretending to search successfully while hitting blocks, CAPTCHAs, or fake pages. It demonstrates how real web access via Bright Data's Web MCP improves reliability, citation quality, and reduces hallucinations compared to fallback to training data.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA demonstrated real-time video diffusion on Blackwell B200 by reducing denoising steps from 50 to as few as one through quantization, latent caching, and step distillation. The approach uses both trajectory-based and distribution-based student model training to match teacher outputs with 10 to 200 times speedup.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use automated loops that prompt agents iteratively rather than direct prompting, reducing manual intervention. This approach scales with orchestrator-worker patterns where an orchestrator spawns parallel agent tasks, storing traces in a database with observability dashboards for monitoring.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding models (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers. It enables routing tasks to different providers based on capability, includes a governance layer for human-in-the-loop oversight, and provides cross-device session continuity.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the apparent shutdown of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model by US government order three days after launch, citing export-control directives and a claimed jailbreak. He covers the regulatory action, performance benchmarks he conducted before the pulldown, and implications for developers building on large language models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's open-source Agents CLI enables developers to build and deploy production-grade AI agents with minimal effort. The tool provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and works with any AI coding assistant including Claude.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is a tool for setting up and building AI agents. The video provides a tutorial and GitHub template for implementation, along with a workshop in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A YouTube video explaining how to construct proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using AI loops. Covers practical examples like autonomous ads optimization and SEO, with references to Loopany tool and memory systems that enable these capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and related tools for autonomous coding workflows. The video covers /goal setup, prompt techniques, goal-buddy, and /Mission exploration for long-horizon task execution.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, and setup procedures including harness configuration and automated testing. It appears to be part of a broader ecosystem that includes Crewlet and Superdesign.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT explained visually..
videos.summary: A video by Caleb Writes Code providing a visual walkthrough of how GPT models work under the hood. The video covers core transformer components including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, token embeddings, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Tutorial explaining Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model implementation covering tokenization, embedding, loss functions, and optimization. Serves as foundational introduction to language model mechanics before moving to attention and GPT architectures.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series of models in three variants: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid mamba transformer architecture, MTP (Multi-Token Prediction), and Latent Mixture of Experts techniques.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released the M3 model, which shifts from full attention to sparse attention via MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA). The model includes tiled and I/O improvements for KV cache optimization, allowing continuous single-pass reads and reducing latency in both prefill and decode inference stages.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a tool that equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS service documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It improves agent success rates and reduces effort by providing structured access to AWS knowledge beyond general training data.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool by Anthropic for building features, fixing bugs, and automating development tasks. AWS Developers demonstrate how to run Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock to keep data within AWS infrastructure while maintaining compliance and cost tracking. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, team cost tracking, and content governance with Bedrock Guardrails.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job market strategies in the age of AI, covering networking, personal branding through blogging and open source contributions, and dismissing concerns that AI will replace junior developers by analogy to prior technological shifts. The video argues that building visible projects and a learning portfolio remains valuable for standing out amid AI-assisted resume screening.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS tutorial covering five production deployment patterns for AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsive UIs, layered authentication with OAuth and IAM separation, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video discussing whether Google engineers use AI code generation tools, comparing approaches from OpenAI Codex and Claude, and speculating on trends at Google in 2024-2026. The video appears to examine the adoption and "vibing" (usage/comfort) with AI-assisted coding across the industry.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company presented as disruptive to open source software development. The video examines how Malus operates and debates whether its business model or practices pose a threat to traditional open source maintenance models.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code is only one component of a software moat; competitive advantage comes from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational scale. He contends that concerns about AI eliminating SaaS companies are overstated and that the best AI products build passionate customer bases rather than mere satisfaction.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a near-term ChatGPT-like moment in robotics is coming, but the field's focus on humanoid form factors like those from 1X and Unitree is myopic. She contends that designing robots for human delight and interaction, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter everyday life.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against much larger enterprises by building a full platform from day one instead of shipping single features. She shares insights on enterprise sales strategy, the importance of customer trust and social proof from respected companies, and a hiring philosophy that focuses on revealing true capability through rigorous evaluation processes.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers are not reliable signals of product-market fit and warns founders against mistaking enthusiasm for real demand. He contends that true PMF manifests as customer pull (demand for pricing) rather than push, and recommends embedded customer relationships over formal interviews as a validation approach.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Agents VS Basic Chatbots: The FUTURE is HERE! #shorts
videos.summary: The video argues that AI agents like Claude outperform traditional RAG chatbots on complex tasks such as file management, spreadsheet creation, and communications. It frames this capability gap as representing the future direction of AI productivity tools.

videos.title: Anthropic vs. Normal Companies: Speed of Innovation Revealed! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that nimble startups like Anthropic outpace large corporations through organizational agility rather than just innovation capability. The advantage stems from lack of legacy systems and rigid employee structures that slow down larger firms.

videos.title: AI's BIGGEST Problem: The Adoption Gap Explained! #shorts
videos.summary: The adoption gap in AI is identified as a major problem where cutting-edge systems like agentic AI and LLMs advance rapidly but businesses struggle to integrate them. The gap is widening, leaving many companies behind in deployment.

videos.title: Local AI vs Cloud: Why Your Excel Data Shouldn't Go To Azure! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that sensitive data in Excel should remain on-premises rather than sent to cloud services like Azure for AI integration. The recommendation emphasizes privacy preservation and risk avoidance through local model deployment.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
